description.title: Joint equilibrium of two coupled carbon markets (THE flagship, converging)
description.economic_framing: Two carbon markets, ETS_A and ETS_B, that share abatement technologies and fuel supply chains, so each market's marginal abatement cost RISES with the OTHER market's allowance price. This mutual dependence makes the two prices a genuine JOINT (simultaneous) equilibrium: neither market is exogenous to the other, so the price pair is found by a fixed-point iteration over the coupled system rather than solved once in sequence.
description.mechanism: Each market is an interior single-block threshold market that pins at its (link-shifted) block cost, so own-price pass-through s_m = 1 and each clears at P_m = c_m + phi_m * P_neighbour. Two mac_cost links close the loop: B->A carries phi_A=0.4 * P_B into A, A->B carries phi_B=0.5 * P_A into B. Loop gain g = phi_A * phi_B = 0.20 (< 1), so the joint solver's damped Gauss-Seidel converges. This mirrors anchor J1 of docs/joint-equilibrium.md.
description.hand_solution: P_A = (c_A + phi_A*c_B)/(1 - g) = (100 + 0.4*80)/0.8 = 165.0 USD/tCO2; P_B = (c_B + phi_B*c_A)/(1 - g) = (80 + 0.5*100)/0.8 = 162.5 USD/tCO2. One Gauss-Seidel sweep does NOT reach this (the one-way seed is P_A=100, P_B=130); iteration is required.
description.joint_solver: relaxation (w) = 0.5 damped Gauss-Seidel; tolerance 1e-12 (tighter than the 1e-4 default so the stopped iterate matches the hand fixed point to 1e-6); max_iterations 200.

expected_output.reading: The joint solver reaches the hand fixed point (165.0, 162.5) and stamps Joint Converged=1 with no cycle. Each cyclic-SCC market row carries the four Joint* diagnostic columns.
